title: Admin Recovery Patch: /admin/upgrade route + upgradeCurrentUserToAdmin backend function

requirements.summary: Add a hidden /admin/upgrade route accessible by direct URL only for authenticated users to upgrade their role to admin using a secret code, with success redirect to /admin/dashboard and error display on invalid code
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating directly to `/admin/upgrade` while logged in shows the Admin Upgrade page with the title, input field, and button.
Unauthenticated users navigating to `/admin/upgrade` are redirected to the landing page (consistent with existing auth guard behavior).
Entering code NAMMA5600 and clicking "Upgrade to Admin" calls the backend function, and on success redirects to `/admin/dashboard`.
Entering an incorrect code displays the error message "Invalid admin code" on the page.
No link, button, or reference to `/admin/upgrade` is added anywhere in the existing UI (Navigation, LandingPage, dashboards, modals, etc.).
The existing `/admin/dashboard` route guard (requires role == admin) is not modified.
No other existing pages, components, or logic are changed.

file_operations.description: Create a new page component AdminUpgradePage that renders a centered card with title "Admin Upgrade", a text input labelled "Enter Admin Setup Code", and a button labelled "Upgrade to Admin". On button click, call the backend upgradeCurrentUserToAdmin function with the entered code. On success (non-null return), invalidate the user profile query and redirect to /admin/dashboard using TanStack Router navigation. On failure (null return or error), display the error message "Invalid admin code" below the input field. Include loading state during the backend call and disable the button while loading. Use existing shadcn/ui components (Card, Input, Button) for consistent styling.

Add a new route configuration for /admin/upgrade in the TanStack Router setup. The route must use an auth guard that only checks if the user is logged in (identity exists) and redirects unauthenticated users to the landing page. Do not check the user's role. Import and render the AdminUpgradePage component at this route. Place this route registration alongside the existing dashboard routes but ensure it does not appear in any navigation menus or UI elements.

Add a new mutation hook useUpgradeToAdmin that calls the backend upgradeCurrentUserToAdmin function with a code parameter. The mutation should return the result string (success message) or null (on error). On success, invalidate the 'currentUserProfile' query key to refresh the user's profile data. Include proper error handling and use the existing actor instance from useActor hook.
